mock.seedUserMessage: How can OneWorks help me take over AI coding work?
mock.seedAssistantMessage: You can choose Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, and other coding agents from one app. Download the desktop app when you want to connect a real local project, terminal, or IM channel.
mock.downloadReply: This is the homepage preview environment. Download the desktop app to connect your local service, orchestrate Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, and other coding agents, then keep working from IM or another device. [Download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}})
mock.downloadReplyCodex: I am Codex, best for turning requirements into code changes, tests, and shippable results. This is still the homepage preview; [download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}}) to connect a real project and keep executing.
mock.downloadReplyClaudeCode: I am Claude Code, best for reading project structure deeply, refactoring across files, and handling complex code changes. This preview will not write files; [download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}}) to connect a real workspace.
mock.downloadReplyKimi: I am Kimi, best for Chinese long-context work, requirement cleanup, and IM-side summaries. This preview only simulates collaboration; [download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}}) to connect real projects and chat surfaces.
mock.downloadReplyGemini: I am Gemini, best for combining screenshots, page state, and code context during analysis. The homepage preview will not access your real files; [download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}}) to connect a local environment.
mock.downloadReplyCopilot: I am Copilot, best for day-to-day coding assistance, completions, and small IDE-style steps. This is the homepage preview; [download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}}) to connect your real project and local tools.
mock.downloadReplyOpencode: I am OpenCode, best for open toolchains and local automation flows. This conversation is simulated; real command execution and file edits require the desktop app. [Download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}})
mock.claudeCodeModelDescription: Best for planning, refactors, and large codebase collaboration
mock.codexModelDescription: Best for fast implementation, verification, and delivery
mock.kimiModelDescription: Best for Chinese context and long-context collaboration
mock.geminiModelDescription: Best for multimodal and toolchain exploration
mock.copilotModelDescription: Best for IDE assistance and day-to-day coding
mock.opencodeModelDescription: Best for open toolchains and local workflows
mock.toolSessionIntro: I will split the work into parallel checks and inspect the frontend project shape first.
mock.toolSessionSummary: The quick checks are done: adapters, IM bridge, and multi-device handoff can all be shown in the preview.
mock.todoAuditAdapters: Confirm Claude Code / Codex / Kimi adapter status
mock.todoConnectIm: Connect Lark, Discord, and QQ channels
mock.toolTodosResult: Task list synced into the preview session.
mock.bashToolDescription: Run a quick preview health check
mock.secondaryUserMessage: Split the adapter area and device switcher into clearer components.
mock.secondaryAssistantIntro: I will read the related components and search where adapters are used.
mock.secondaryAssistantSummary: AgentDock, DeviceSwitcher, and the adapter data layer can be split so the homepage is easier to animate and make responsive.
mock.imUserMessage: Mirror task progress into Lark, Discord, and QQ so users can continue from any chat surface.
mock.imAssistantIntro: I will simulate checking the webhook status for the three channels.
mock.imAssistantSummary: All three IM channels are in preview mode. In the real app, the desktop client connects your local service and real credentials.
mock.roomLastMessage: Claude Code is waiting for confirmation before continuing local execution.
mock.roomHostLatestSummary: Coordinating planning, implementation, and verification progress.
mock.roomHostUserMessage: Split a complex development task across different AI coding partners.
mock.roomHostAssistantMessage: The orchestration workspace is ready: Codex owns the execution plan, Claude Code prepares code changes, and Kimi keeps summaries in sync. The preview only shows collaboration state and will not write real files.
mock.roomCodexLatestSummary: Execution plan and task split are complete.
mock.roomClaudeLatestSummary: Waiting for confirmation before continuing local execution.
mock.roomAssignmentCodex: Assigned Codex to organize the goal, project context, and execution order.
mock.roomAttentionClaude: Claude Code wants to continue local code changes. The preview will not write files; install the desktop app for real execution.
mock.roomCompletedCodex: Codex finished the requirements breakdown and execution plan.
mock.roomApprovalInstallDescription: Download the desktop app to connect the local project and run real changes.
mock.roomApprovalPreviewDescription: Stay in the homepage preview without writing local files.
mock.roomCodexUserMessage: Break this complex task into an executable plan first.
mock.roomCodexAssistantIntro: I split the goal into execution phases, risk checks, and verification paths.
mock.roomCodexTodoOne: Confirm task goals and project context
mock.roomCodexTodoTwo: Confirm implementation, verification, and delivery order
mock.roomClaudeUserMessage: Prepare implementation and verification without affecting the existing project.
mock.roomClaudeAssistantIntro: I am preparing code changes and verification steps. The preview shows intent but does not write real files.
mock.roomClaudeResult: Preview execution intent generated. Real writes require the desktop app.
mock.roomDownloadReply: This is the homepage preview orchestration workspace. To let agents write files, start terminals, or connect IM for real, [download OneWorks]({{downloadUrl}}).
mock.roomInteractionHandled: Your choice was recorded. Real execution requires the One Works desktop app.
mock.roomNotFound: Preview orchestration workspace was not found
mock.terminalInstallNotice:
The homepage preview does not start a real terminal or write your files.
Download the One Works desktop app to connect your local project, run tasks, and control a real terminal: {{downloadUrl}}
mock.workspaceReadmeIntro: This is a read-only frontend project in the homepage preview, used to show the file tree, context references, and task collaboration.
mock.workspaceFileNotFound: The preview project does not contain this file: {{path}}
mock.readonlyWorkspaceMessage: The homepage preview is read-only. To edit files, download the One Works desktop app and connect your local project: {{downloadUrl}}
mock.specOnboardingDescription: Route user intent across Codex, Claude Code, Kimi, and other adapters while syncing preview state.
mock.specParamAdapter: Target coding agent, such as Codex or Claude Code.
mock.specParamChannel: The surface the user is on, such as desktop, browser, or IM.
mock.specOnboardingBody:
### Goal

Use one app to choose adapters, split tasks, sync messages, and hand work off across devices.

### Preview behavior

- Only simulated state is shown.
- File and terminal operations stay inside the homepage preview.
mock.specImDescription: Simulate task summaries going to Lark, Discord, and QQ so the user can continue from chat.
mock.specParamSummary: The task summary sent to IM.
mock.specImBody:
### Triggers

When the orchestration workspace has pending confirmation, completed work, or a handoff request, the preview creates an IM-style message.

### Real use

After installing the desktop app, OneWorks connects your real project and real IM credentials.
mock.entityCodexDescription: Moves tasks quickly through implementation, verification, and delivery.
mock.entityCodexBody: Codex acts as an executor in the preview, showing tool calls, task lists, and verification output.
mock.entityKimiDescription: Handles Chinese-language long-context understanding and IM-side summaries.
mock.entityKimiBody: Kimi is used in the preview to show summaries and handoff across chat surfaces.
mock.ruleReadonlyDescription: File writes, terminal execution, and external credentials all remain simulated.
mock.ruleReadonlyBody:
This preview environment will not modify your local files.

To write files, run terminals, or connect a local project for real, download OneWorks: {{downloadUrl}}
mock.ruleDownloadDescription: Local projects, terminals, IM credentials, and device handoff require the desktop app.
mock.ruleDownloadBody:
The homepage only shows the basic interaction model.

After installing the desktop app, OneWorks can connect the local service, read project context, and execute real tasks: {{downloadUrl}}
mock.skillAdapterRoutingDescription: Choose Codex, Claude Code, Kimi, and other agents based on task shape.
mock.skillAdapterRoutingBody: Shows multi-adapter routing: planning can go to long-context agents, while implementation and verification go to execution-focused agents.
mock.skillImBridgeDescription: Push orchestration events, pending confirmations, and task summaries into chat surfaces.
mock.skillImBridgeBody: The homepage preview only generates simulated messages. In real use, the desktop app connects Lark, Discord, QQ, and other channels.
mock.skillMultiDeviceDescription: Continue the same task context from desktop, browser, or another device.
mock.skillMultiDeviceBody: Shows that OneWorks does not tie work to one computer. The preview only displays device state and does not open real connections.
mock.workspaceAssetDescription: A read-only frontend project for showing the file tree, context references, and task collaboration.
mock.skillCreatedDescription: A simulated Skill created inside the homepage preview.
mock.skillCreatedBody: The homepage preview does not write a real Skill. To save it into your project, download OneWorks: {{downloadUrl}}
mock.skillHubRegistryDescription: A built-in simulated registry for showing the Skill Market interaction.
mock.skillHubUiReviewDescription: Review homepage visuals, responsive layout, and accessibility issues.
mock.skillHubAdapterSmokeDescription: Run a small adapter smoke check and produce a readable summary.
mock.skillHubImHandoffDescription: Simulate IM handoff, room summaries, and pending confirmation notices.
mock.knowledgeItemNotFound: This homepage preview data asset was not found
